# Library of climate resources

![](/climate-library/Content/Images/banner.jpg)

This is a collection of links to climate datasets, tools, guidance and related resources. The sources include the federal government, provincial and territorial governments, national professional organizations, climate consortia and established international organizations. It can be useful for impact, vulnerability and risk assessments, and for adaptation planning.  
  
Refine your search using the search filters. You can also type up to 5 keywords in the search bar.

If you have questions, [contact the Climate Services Support Desk.](http://climate-change.canada.ca/support-desk)

--- SOURCE 5: Building Hope Through Climate Change Action ---
URL: http://www.bccdc.ca/pop-public-health/Documents/Building_Hope_Through_Climate_Action.pdf

SUMMARY:
Many more communities in BC have experienced first hand the effects of climate change on their lives and there are many more case studies that can be shared,



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://www.c2es.org/site/assets/uploads/2007/12/regional-impacts-climate-change-four-case-studies-united-states-cover.png
DESCRIPTION: The cover shows the title "Regional Impacts of climate change" with the subtitle "Four Case Studies in the United States" along with the authors' names and logos of the Pew Center on Global Climate Change.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://www.mdpi.com/climate/climate-04-00039/article_deploy/html/images/climate-04-00039-g001.png
DESCRIPTION: This diagram illustrates the process of integrating global and regional climate models to assess climate change impacts on hydrology, including statistical downscaling and bias correction techniques, and the subsequent evaluation of future runoff, water resources, and flooding scenarios.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org/bitstream/handle/10986/30932/WBG_Africa_Low_Carbon.pdf.jpg?sequence=5&isAllowed=y
DESCRIPTION: None

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://www.pnas.org/cms/10.1073/pnas.2205512120/asset/bbf5a527-e3fc-4d7e-ada7-1542950bc13e/assets/images/large/pnas.2205512120fig02.jpg
DESCRIPTION: None

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://www.frontiersin.org/files/Articles/1037117/fclim-05-1037117-HTML/image_m/fclim-05-1037117-g006.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates the impact chain of drought and low water levels in the Rhine on various sectors, detailing associated hazards, risks, and adaptation measures pertaining to ecology, energy, water management, and logistics in the Mannheim region.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Analysis focuses on local climate data, identifying unique vulnerabilities and adaptation needs in regions like the Prairie and Red Deer regions, as highlighted in Canadian case studies.

Comparative evaluation of climate models against observed data reveals discrepancies, emphasizing the need for improved modeling techniques and data integration for accurate regional climate predictions.

Case studies reveal essential insights into effective adaptation strategies, highlighting the importance of community engagement and tailored approaches to address specific regional climate challenges.
